Package ru.nsu.kiryushin.controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
ru.nsu.kiryushin.controller.GameController
public class GameController extends Object
Mediates between the view and the Game model. Holds no JavaFX dependencies.

The view interacts with the game only through this class and a small set of basic value types (Point, Direction, GameState, food type names), so it never needs to import the model's own classes (Game, Snake, Food).

  • Constructor Details

    • GameController

      public GameController()
      Creates a controller with default board parameters.

    • GameController

      public GameController(int columns, int rows, int winLength, int targetFoodCount)
      Creates a controller with the given board parameters.
      Parameters:
      columns - board width in tiles
      rows - board height in tiles
      winLength - snake length required to win
      targetFoodCount - number of food items kept on the board

  • Method Details

    • restart

      public void restart()
      Starts a new game, replacing the current one.

    • handleInput

      public void handleInput(Direction direction)
      Routes a direction command to the snake.
      Parameters:
      direction - requested direction

    • update

      public boolean update()
      Advances one game tick.
      Returns:
      true if the game is still running, false if it has ended

    • getColumns

      public int getColumns()
      Returns:
      board width in tiles

    • getRows

      public int getRows()
      Returns:
      board height in tiles

    • getState

      public GameState getState()
      Returns:
      current game state

    • getScore

      public int getScore()
      Returns:
      current snake length / score

    • getSpeedMultiplier

      public double getSpeedMultiplier()
      Returns:
      current speed multiplier (1.0 = normal)

    • getTickInterval

      public double getTickInterval()
      Returns:
      nanoseconds per tick at the current speed

    • getSnakeDirection

      public Direction getSnakeDirection()
      Returns:
      current movement direction of the snake

    • getSnakeBody

      public List<Point> getSnakeBody()
      Returns the snake body ordered head-to-tail.
      Returns:
      list of body cells

    • getFoodsOnBoard

      public Map<Point,String> getFoodsOnBoard()
      Returns the food currently on the board, mapping each cell to a stable food type name (the lowercase simple class name, e.g. "apple"). The view uses this name to pick a texture without depending on Food.
      Returns:
      map of cell to food type name

    • getFoodTypeNames

      public List<String> getFoodTypeNames()
      Returns the names of all known food types (lowercase simple class names). The view uses this to load the matching textures up front.
      Returns:
      list of food type names
